summary: Scarlet Begonias > Fire ignites a high‑energy second set with tight jams
blurb: The Sept 15 1985 show delivers an unforgettable Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain sequence and a driving U.S. Blues finale. Jerry’s soaring leads, Brent’s warm keyboard textures, and a powerful pre‑drums/Space segment keep the momentum rolling. Overall the performance is tight, cohesive, and full of classic Dead excitement.
review: On September 15, 1985 at Devore Field, Southwestern College, the Dead rolled out a setlist that crackles from start to finish. After a brisk first set anchored by Alabama Getaway and Deal, the second set erupts with a Twilight Zone intro that segues straight into one of the era’s most celebrated Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain transitions. Jerry Garcia’s guitar sings sweetly over Brent Mydland’s “sepia bubbles,” while Phil Lesh lays down a low‑end “down comforter” that anchors the band through Samson & Delilah, She Belongs To Me and an expansive jam that weaves Truckin’ into Comes A Time and Around & Around. The pre‑drums/Space segment builds tension before bursting into a high‑octane U.S. Blues that rolls straight into a jubilant (I Can’t Get No) Satisfaction, ending with a heartfelt Brokedown Palace encore. Across the multiple recordings, reviewers consistently praise the warm, well‑balanced soundboard captures and note the show’s experimental yet cohesive flow, making this night a standout entry in the mid‑80s catalog.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: The majority of recordings (5 out of 9) are marked positive, with additional mixed reviews still highlighting strong performance aspects and only one negative review focused on recording quality rather than the show itself. Consistent praise for Scarlet>Fire, She Belongs To Me, and the second‑set energy yields a robust positive sentiment.

best_recording.reason: Warm, well‑recorded soundboard with clear balance and tube‑fuzz tone; captures the iconic Scarlet > Fire and U.S. Blues moments without dropouts.

key_highlights: Unforgettable Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain sequence
High‑energy second set featuring a tight pre‑drums/Space segue into U.S. Blues and Satisfaction
Strong She Belongs To Me performance with emotive vocals
Warm, cohesive flow highlighted by the Twilight Zone intro

band_performance.Jerry: Soaring, sweet leads on Scarlet > Fire and Samson; wailing guitar throughout with only a minor vocal slip on Comes A Time.
band_performance.Phil: Solid low‑end foundation, hot driving bass especially on Scarlet > Fire and Satisfaction; described as a "down comforter" for the band.
band_performance.Bob: Rhythm work praised on Smokestack Lightning and first‑set jams; a single flub noted in Truckin' but otherwise steady.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboard textures likened to "sepia bubbles," delivering powerful solos and warm support that lift the entire set.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums add high‑jinks and drive the U.S. Blues groove; intense, unpredictable moments during the pre‑drums/Space segment.
band_performance.Billy: Provides tight, energetic drumming that locks in with Mickey, especially during the powerful second‑set climax.
